calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Accounting And Sale Of The Property
Off Calendar Apr-02-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christina Nelson for and with Michael Tong
Paul Melbostad for Richard Tong (Co-Trustee/Beneficiary) and Richard Swartzbaugh (Beneficiary)

Counsel reported parties have reached a settlement agreement which has been signed, and would like the Court to approve it. The Court informed Counsel that they need to file a petition for approval of the settlement agreement. Status of Accounting and Sale of Real Property is Off Calendar.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Accounting And Sale Of The Property
Off Calendar Feb-05-2018 Continued To Apr-02-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christins Nelson for petitioner Michael Tong; Paul Melbostad for objector Richard Tong.

Ms. Nelson stated that the proposed sale of the property for $908,000 fell through, and that the back-up offer of $850,000 was accepted. She stated that the escrow documents do not provide for payout of capital gains taxes or other estate charges. She stated that she had sent a settlement offer to Mr. melbostad, but had received nothing in response. Mr. Melbostad responded that there was a willing buyer prepared to close based upon the $850.000 purchase price, and that the parties could work out their differences. He stated that his client was willing to meet with Michael Tong, and proposed that the hearing be continued for 30 days to give the two sides time to meet and attempt to work out an agreement. In response to an inquiry from the Court, he acknowledged that an accounting by his client was ordered by the Court and has not been done, but added that there were issues between the parties that would not be resolved by the accounting and that he thought the parties should meet to discuss their differences. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 23, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 28, 2018. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on April 2, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation and status of the sale of the property.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of The Accounting And Sale Of The Property
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Continued To Feb-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Paul Melbostad.
Donald Drummond and Christina Nelson for and with Michael Tong.

Mr. Melbostad reported that the Co-Trustees accepted an offer of $908,000.00 for sale of the TIC Unit 252 property on Saturday, stated that he sent a letter last week/proposal for reimbursement to Rick Swartzbaugh for funds advanced for administration and raised the issue of not paying funds to an accountant if settled. Ms. Nelson stated that they made a proposal to settle, that escrow closes in seven days, and that the money should remain in escrow until there is an accounting or settlement is done. Status of the accounting, etc. is continued to February 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition For Orders (1) Compelling Trustees To List And Sell Real Property And (2) Removing Trustee; Petition For Interim Order Suspending Trustee;Etc
Off Calendar Sep-15-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christina Nelson and Donald Drummond for and with Michael Tong (Petitioner).
Paul Melbostad for and with Richard Tong and Richard Swartzbaugh (Respondents).

Counsel met with the Judicial Officer in chambers and then proceeded outside of the courtroom to work on settlement. Upon returning, the Parties presented a stipulation and order pursuant to agreement (signed in open Court). The Petition for Orders Compelling Trustees to List and Sell Real Property is off calendar. Status of the accounting and sale of the property is set for December 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Barrientos. Not reported.

Petition For Orders (1) Compelling Trustees To List And Sell Real Property And (2) Removing Trustee; Petition For Interim Order Suspending Trustee;Etc
Off Calendar Jul-21-2017 Continued To Sep-15-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orders (1) Compelling Trustees To List And Sell Real Property And (2) Removing Trustee; Petition For Interim Order Suspending Trustee;Etc
Off Calendar Jun-29-2017 Continued To Jul-21-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The matter is submitted.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

Petition For Orders (1) Compelling Trustees To List And Sell Real Property And (2) Removing Trustee; Petition For Interim Order Suspending Trustee;Etc
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Continued To Jun-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christina Gallagher for Michael Tong.
Paul Melbostad for Richard Tong and Rick Swarzbaugh.

Mr. Melbostad stated that he has not filed objections, that from day one his client has been in agreement to the sale of the TIC unit 252, that he received a list of reimbursements that Ms. Gallagher's client is requesting, that his clients have agreed to a significant amount and that they have a Broker who is willing to advance $87,000.00 for improvements of the property for which his client will be responsible for. Ms. Gallagher stated that she got a message from a broker in December indicating that a contract would be ready in January, that they were showed a list of what was to be done, that the tenants were to be out by January and that nothing has been done. Ms. Gallagher also stated that the mediation deadline passed, that Richard entered into a rental lease agreement in April, that she received a one page list which is not an accounting, that she received a settlement that was marked up and that she feels they have been strung along. In addition, Ms. Gallagher stated that her Client wants his bequest and reimbursements and that she is prepared for this case to go forward. The Court stated that it is prepared to take the matter under submission as of June 12, 2017 and that what ever information Mr. Melbostad wants to present in the form of an objection is to be filed no later than June 5, 2017. If nothing is filed, the petition will be deemed un-objected to. The Petition for Orders Compelling Trustee to List and Sell Real Property, etc. is continued to June 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Orders (1) Compelling Trustees To List And Sell Real Property And (2) Removing Trustee; Petition For Interim Order Suspending Trustee;Etc
Off Calendar Apr-10-2017 Continued To Apr-24-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orders (1) Compelling Trustees To List And Sell Real Property And (2) Removing Trustee; Petition For Interim Order Suspending Trustee;Etc
Off Calendar Dec-28-2016 Continued To Apr-10-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christina Nelson for (co-trustee & beneficiary) Michael Tong. Paul Melbostad for
Richard Tong.

Mr. Melbostad reported that he has confirmed with the real estate agent that they have agreed on a listing, that they are waiting for the contractor's proposal, that it is the broker and contractor that are holding things up and requested a 60 day continuance. Ms. Nelson stated that the proposal has strong potential without a mediator but that things have been dragging on, that there are long standing family issues and that they are okay with the 60 day continuance but that is it. Mr. Melbostad stated that they are only interested in proceeding with the sale and that they are not intending to litigate. Ms. Nelson addressed the probate examiner's notes stating that in regards to notice the code is not applicable to this petition, that regarding the issue of legal description 252-254 26th Avenue is the physical address, that the address on the deed is the mailing address only, that it is only one house and that the description is correct. Mr. Melbostad confirmed the same. The Court instructed Ms. Nelson to file a declaration with this information and set a deadline for filing objections of February 27, 2017 and a mediation deadline of March 27, 2017. The Petition for orders compelling trustees to list and sell real property, etc., was continued to April 10, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
